sections.paragraph:
ἀποκομίζω fut. ptc. acc. ἀποκομιοῦντας 2 Macc. 2:15; 1 aor. subj. ἀποκομίσω (s. κομίζω; Hdt. et al.; ins, pap, LXX; GrBar 11:9; Joseph.; apolog.; Theoph. Ant. 3, 25 [p. 258, 9]) take along (Polyb. 28, 10, 7) γράμματα a letter Pol 13:1.—DELG s.v. κομέω 2. κομίζω.

fut. fut. = fut.

ptc. ptc. = participle

acc. acc. = accusative

aor. aor. = aorist

subj. subj. = subjunctive; subject

Hdt. Hdt. = Herodotus, V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

ins Ins, ins = Inscription, Inschrift, inscription(s). Without a period, esp. in lists, as at the beginning of entries; the capitalized form is used in titles. In conjunction with literary works this abbr. refers to the title or description of contents.

pap pap = papyrus, -yri

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

GrBar GrBar = Greek Baruch (Apocalypsis Baruchi Graece)—List 2

Joseph Joseph This abbr. used when no specific texts are cited (s. Jos.); I a.d.—List 5

apolog. apolog. = apologist(s)

Theoph. Ant. Theoph. Ant. = Theophilus Antiochenus, II a.d.—List 5

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 113
